examples: The XML document is checked against a very restrictive schema.
This reduces the effort to write and maintain the schema, as well as ensures that no complex type is accidentally left inextensible.
Willy-nilly, no-schema, non-organized data is a recipe for disaster!
The schema-less framework is not the most efficient solution for analyzing data where relationships matter.
Up-front schema design is a poor fit in a world where data requirements are fluid.
